name: Eleanor Roosevelt
description: American diplomat and activist, First Lady of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1884–1962)
abstract: Anna Eleanor Roosevelt ( EL-in-or ROH-zə-velt; October 11, 1884 – November 7, 1962) was an American political figure, diplomat, and activist. She was the longest-serving first lady of the United States, during her husband Franklin D.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I became close friends with Eleanor Roosevelt and spent a lot of time with her in my early days.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Eleanor Roosevelt with Marion Anderson, but they weren’t doing anything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Eleanor Roosevelt, Mrs. Franklin Delano Roosevelt was there, and she was to make a speech later... I politely turned around to Mrs. Roosevelt and asked her did she mind moving

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I'm standing in between Eleanor Roosevelt and Orson Welles... when I spoke with Mrs. Roosevelt

about_quotes.speaker: Jean Bach
about_quotes.evidence: He told everybody that Eleanor Roosevelt and I were his two favorite writers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jean Bach interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995
